checks.tests: PASS — 2469 tests across 202 files, all passing

bulk_update_zod_validation.detail: SEC-R6-01 fixed: POST /tasks/bulk/update now runs UpdateTaskInput.safeParse(changes) before passing to updateMany. Invalid payloads return 400 with flattened Zod errors. Import confirmed at line 5.

settings_value_type_check.detail: SEC-R6-02 fixed: PUT /settings/:key now checks typeof body.value !== 'string' and returns 400 before writing. Eliminates the unsafe `as string` cast.

warnings_addressed_this_round: SEC-R6-01 — POST /tasks/bulk/update now validates changes with Zod (FIXED)
SEC-R6-02 — PUT /settings/:key now type-checks value is string (FIXED)
warnings_remaining_for_future: QUAL-R6-01 — POST /plugins/timeblocking/rpc has no per-method arg validation (73-line switch, medium risk, plugin-internal only)
suggestions_deferred: QUAL-R6-02 — React context memoization patterns confirmed clean (no action needed)
SEC-R6-03 — /tasks/reorder and /tasks/import lack body validation (low, has per-item try/catch)
SEC-R6-04 — AI/plugin endpoints use `as` casts instead of Zod (low, internal-facing)
DX-R6-01 — AppProviders context ordering confirmed correct (no action needed)
DX-R6-02 — PERF-301 TODO for batch settings endpoint (pre-existing, already annotated)
PERF-R6-01 — Markdown backend updateManyTasks is serial (expected for filesystem backend)

notes: Round 6 was a deep sweep with minimal findings, consistent with a maturing codebase after 5 prior audit rounds. The two security warnings (bulk update validation, settings type check) were fixed correctly — both verified via git diff and confirmed passing typecheck + full test suite. The remaining warning (timeblocking RPC dispatcher) is legitimate but low-urgency: it is plugin-internal, only reachable from the timeblocking UI, and the switch cases are finite. All 5 suggestions are either confirmations of correct patterns or known low-priority items. No regressions introduced.
